info.title: Universal Document AI API
info.description: AI-powered document editing with multi-tenant organization support

post.description:
Send a message to SuperDocs AI and receive an AI response with optional document edits.

The AI agent can edit, search, create, and delete document sections based on your message.
Provide document_html to give the AI context about the current document state.
Returns the AI response text and any document changes (HTML updates, creates, deletes).
Each message that triggers a document action counts as one billable operation.

Optional parameters:
- model_tier: AI model to use ('core', 'turbo', 'pro', 'max'). Default: 'core'.
- thinking_depth: Reasoning depth ('fast', 'balanced', 'deep'). Default: auto-selected.
- approval_mode: Change review mode ('approve_all' or 'ask_every_time'). Default: 'approve_all'.
- image_attachments: Inline images for vision-based analysis.

When approval_mode is 'ask_every_time', the response includes pending changes
requiring review via the async workflow (use chat_async instead for full HITL support).

get.description:
List all document editing sessions for the authenticated user.

Returns session IDs, message counts, timestamps, and preview text.
Use a session_id from this list when calling the chat or history endpoints.

parameters.name: user_id

schema.description: Filter by user ID (deprecated - auth determines scope)
schema.title: User Id

parameters.description: Filter by user ID (deprecated - auth determines scope)

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Maximum number of sessions to return
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Maximum number of sessions to return

get.description:
Get conversation history and document state for a specific session.

Returns the full message history (user and AI messages), current document HTML content,
document version, chunk count, and attachment list. Use this to restore a previous session
or inspect what changes were made.

post.description:
Upload a document attachment to a session for AI reference.

The file is processed asynchronously — text is extracted, converted, and indexed
so the AI can search and reference it during chat. Use GET /v1/attachments/status/{session_id}
to check processing progress.

Supported file types: .pdf, .docx, .txt, .rtf, .md (max 50 MB).
Returns a job_id for tracking the processing status.

delete.description:
Remove an attachment from a session or cancel its processing.

If the attachment is still being processed, cancels the processing job.
If processing completed, removes the attachment from the session.
The attachment_id can be either the final attachment ID or the job ID (during processing).

get.description:
Get all attachments and their processing status for a session.

Returns ready attachments (with metadata like filename, type, size, chunk count)
and in-progress processing jobs. Use this to check if an uploaded attachment is ready
for the AI to reference.

post.description: Legacy document upload endpoint - redirects to attachments/upload

get.description:
List all async jobs for the authenticated user.

Returns job IDs, statuses, and results. Use this after calling chat/async
to find your pending or completed jobs. Filter by status (pending, processing, completed, failed).

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Maximum number of jobs to return
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Maximum number of jobs to return

get.description:
Get the status and result of a specific async job.

Poll this endpoint after starting an async chat request.
Possible statuses: pending, in_progress, awaiting_approval, completed, failed, cancelled.

When status is 'awaiting_approval', the metadata.pending_changes array contains
the proposed changes (change_id, operation, chunk_id, old_html, new_html, ai_explanation).
Call POST /v1/chat/{session_id}/approve to approve or deny them.

When status is 'completed', the result field contains the AI response and any document changes.

post.description:
Cancel a pending or in-progress async job.

Only jobs with status 'pending' or 'processing' can be cancelled.
Returns the updated job details with status 'cancelled'.

get.description:
Get all async jobs for a specific session, sorted by most recent first.

Returns job IDs, statuses, and results for all operations in the session.
Useful for checking if any jobs are still running or awaiting approval.

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Maximum number of jobs to return
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Maximum number of jobs to return

post.description:
Start an asynchronous chat request and return a job ID for polling.

Use this for long-running document operations. The request is processed in the background.
Poll the job status using GET /v1/jobs/{job_id} until status is 'completed' or 'failed'.
The result contains the AI response and any document changes, same as the sync chat endpoint.

Optional parameters:
- model_tier: AI model to use ('core', 'turbo', 'pro', 'max'). Default: 'core'.
- thinking_depth: Reasoning depth ('fast', 'balanced', 'deep'). Default: auto-selected.
- approval_mode: Change review mode ('approve_all' or 'ask_every_time'). Default: 'approve_all'.
- image_attachments: Inline images for vision-based analysis.

HITL workflow (approval_mode='ask_every_time'):
1. Poll GET /v1/jobs/{job_id} — status transitions to 'awaiting_approval'
2. Read metadata.pending_changes for proposed edits (change_id, operation, old_html, new_html)
3. POST /v1/chat/{session_id}/approve with approved=true/false per change
4. Continue polling — job resumes and eventually reaches 'completed'

get.description:
Stream real-time progress for a chat job using Server-Sent Events (SSE).

Opens an SSE connection that streams events as the AI processes your request.
Use this with the job_id returned from POST /v1/chat/async.

Event types:
- 'intermediate': Progress updates during processing (content, sequence, timestamp).
- 'proposed_change': A document change proposed for review (when approval_mode is 'ask_every_time').
- 'final': Processing complete. Contains the full result with AI response and document changes.
- 'usage': Billing data emitted after 'final' (monthly_used, monthly_limit, monthly_remaining).
- 'error': An error occurred (job failed, cancelled, or not found).

Authentication: Pass a token or api_key as a query parameter (required for EventSource which cannot set headers).

parameters.name: job_id

schema.description: Job ID to stream progress for
schema.title: Job Id

parameters.description: Job ID to stream progress for

parameters.name: token

schema.description: Authentication token (query parameter for EventSource compatibility)
schema.title: Token

parameters.description: Authentication token (query parameter for EventSource compatibility)

parameters.name: api_key

schema.description: API key (query parameter for EventSource compatibility)
schema.title: Api Key

parameters.description: API key (query parameter for EventSource compatibility)

post.description:
Approve or deny a proposed AI change during human-in-the-loop (HITL) review.

When a job has status 'awaiting_approval', the AI has proposed document changes
that require user review. Poll GET /v1/jobs/{job_id} to see the pending_changes
in the result field, then call this endpoint to approve or deny.

For single changes: set approved=true/false and optionally provide feedback.
For batch changes: provide a 'changes' array with per-change decisions.
After approval, the job resumes processing and eventually reaches 'completed'.

get.description:
Long-poll for real-time job updates on a session (B2B organizations only).

Returns immediately if there are recent job updates, or holds the connection
open up to the specified timeout. Use the 'since' parameter to avoid receiving
duplicate updates.

parameters.name: since

schema.description: ISO 8601 timestamp — only return updates after this time
schema.title: Since

parameters.description: ISO 8601 timestamp — only return updates after this time

parameters.name: timeout

schema.description: Long polling timeout in seconds (max 1800 = 30 minutes)
schema.title: Timeout

parameters.description: Long polling timeout in seconds (max 1800 = 30 minutes)

post.description:
Upload a document file as a personal/organization template.

The file is processed synchronously: text extraction, HTML conversion, and content indexing.
Supported formats: .docx, .pdf, .txt, .rtf, .md
Templates are scoped to the uploading user or organization.

get.description:
List all active templates for the authenticated user or organization.

Returns template metadata including name, file type, size, and creation date.
Templates are scoped — users see only their own templates, organizations see theirs.

delete.description:
Delete a template by ID. Only the owner (user or organization) can delete their templates.

The template is soft-deleted and will no longer appear in list results.

get.description:
Get current user's profile information

Returns user profile with subscription tier and usage limits

patch.description:
Update current user's profile

Allows updating display name, timezone, language, and preferences

get.description:
Get detailed usage statistics for current user

Returns operation counts, token usage, and success rates by operation type

List all chat sessions for current user

Returns list of sessions with last activity and message counts

schema.title: Response Get User Sessions V1 Users Me Sessions Get

delete.description:
Delete a specific chat session

Removes all messages for the given session ID (user must own the session)

get.description:
Check current usage limits and remaining operations

Returns real-time usage information with reset date

List all API keys for the current user (masked).

Returns key prefix and last 4 characters for identification.

schema.title: Response List Api Keys V1 Users Me Api Keys Get

post.description:
Create a new API key for the current user.

The raw key is returned ONCE in the response. It cannot be retrieved again.

delete.description:
Revoke (soft delete) an API key.

The key will be marked as inactive and can no longer be used for authentication.

get.description:
Check if the SuperDocs API is healthy and responsive.

Returns 200 with status 'healthy' if the service is running.
Use this to verify connectivity before making other API calls.

last_four.title: Last Four
last_four.description: Last 4 characters of the key for identification.

is_active.title: Is Active
is_active.description: Whether the key is active. Revoked keys show as false.

created_at.title: Created At
created_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the key was created.

last_used_at.title: Last Used At
last_used_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of last use, or null if never used.

job_id.title: Job Id
job_id.description: Job ID that is awaiting approval.

change_id.title: Change Id
change_id.description: Specific change to approve/deny (for individual decisions). Get change IDs from the job's pending_changes.

approved.title: Approved
approved.description: Whether to approve (true) or deny (false) the change.

feedback.title: Feedback
feedback.description: Optional feedback explaining why a change was denied. Helps the AI adjust future suggestions.

changes.title: Changes
changes.description: Batch decisions: array of {change_id, approved, feedback} objects. Use for 'Accept All' or 'Deny All'.

ApprovalRequest.title: ApprovalRequest
ApprovalRequest.description: Approve or deny proposed AI document changes.

message.title: Message
message.description: Your message to the AI assistant.

session_id.title: Session Id
session_id.description: Unique session identifier. Reuse to continue a conversation.

document_html.title: Document Html
document_html.description: Current document HTML. Include data-chunk-id attributes on elements to enable targeted AI edits.

user_id.title: User Id
user_id.description: User identifier. Automatically set from authentication — typically omit this.

async_mode.title: Async Mode
async_mode.description: Must be true for async processing. Included for backward compatibility.

model_tier.title: Model Tier
model_tier.description: AI model to use: 'core' (default, fast), 'turbo' (fastest), 'pro' (advanced reasoning), 'max' (most capable).

thinking_depth.title: Thinking Depth
thinking_depth.description: Reasoning depth: 'fast', 'balanced' (default), or 'deep'. Controls how much analysis the AI performs.

approval_mode.title: Approval Mode
approval_mode.description: Change review mode: 'approve_all' (default, auto-applies changes) or 'ask_every_time' (pauses for your review).

AsyncChatRequest.title: AsyncChatRequest
AsyncChatRequest.description: Start an async chat request that processes in the background.

job_id.title: Job Id
job_id.description: Job identifier. Poll GET /v1/jobs/{job_id} for status and results.

AsyncChatResponse.title: AsyncChatResponse
AsyncChatResponse.description: Response from starting an async chat request.

document_changes.description: Document modifications made by the AI. Present only when the document was changed.

usage.description: Operation usage data. Present for authenticated users with usage tracking.

ChatResponse.title: ChatResponse
ChatResponse.description: AI response with optional document changes and usage data.

examples.response: I've added a confidentiality clause to section 3 covering non-disclosure obligations.

name.title: Name
name.description: A label for this key (e.g., 'My App', 'CI Pipeline').

id.title: Id
id.description: Key identifier (UUID). Use this to revoke the key later.

name.title: Name
name.description: Label you assigned to this key.

key.title: Key
key.description: The full API key (sk_... format). Copy this now — it cannot be retrieved again.

key_prefix.title: Key Prefix
key_prefix.description: First 7 characters of the key (e.g., 'sk_a1b2') for identification.

created_at.title: Created At
created_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the key was created.

CreateApiKeyResponse.title: CreateApiKeyResponse
CreateApiKeyResponse.description: Newly created API key. The raw key is shown only once — save it immediately.

updated_html.title: Updated Html
updated_html.description: Full updated document HTML with data-chunk-id attributes. Apply this to your editor to sync the document.

version_id.title: Version Id
version_id.description: Document version identifier. Changes on every modification.

changes_summary.title: Changes Summary
changes_summary.description: Human-readable summary of what was changed.

requires_approval.title: Requires Approval
requires_approval.description: If true, changes need review via the approve endpoint before being applied.

pending_changes.title: Pending Changes
pending_changes.description: Proposed changes awaiting approval. Present when requires_approval is true.

changes.title: Changes
changes.description: History of individual changes applied during this request.

DocumentChanges.title: DocumentChanges
DocumentChanges.description: Document modifications produced by the AI assistant.

html_content.title: Html Content
html_content.description: Full document HTML content with data-chunk-id attributes.

chunk_count.title: Chunk Count
chunk_count.description: Number of content sections in the document.

last_modified.title: Last Modified
last_modified.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the last modification.

attachments.title: Attachments
attachments.description: Session attachments. Each item has: id (string), filename (string), file_extension (string), processing_status ('ready', 'processing', or 'failed').

DocumentState.title: DocumentState
DocumentState.description: Current document state for session restoration.

messages.title: Messages
messages.description: Ordered list of messages. Each item has: id (string), sender ('user' or 'ai'), content (string), timestamp (ISO 8601), turn_index (int).

document_state.description: Current document state. Present if the session has an active document.

editor_action.title: Editor Action
editor_action.description: Frontend instruction: 'update' (load document_state HTML into editor), 'clear' (reset editor), or 'keep' (no change).

EnhancedChatHistoryResponse.title: EnhancedChatHistoryResponse
EnhancedChatHistoryResponse.description: Conversation history with document state and restoration instructions.

ImageAttachmentData.title: ImageAttachmentData
ImageAttachmentData.description: Inline image attachment for vision-based analysis.

file_size.title: File Size
file_size.description: File size in bytes (attachment processing jobs).

content_type.title: Content Type
content_type.description: MIME type of uploaded file (attachment processing jobs).

document_html_provided.title: Document Html Provided
document_html_provided.description: Whether document HTML was included in the request (chat jobs).

pending_changes.title: Pending Changes
pending_changes.description: Proposed changes awaiting approval. Present when job status is 'awaiting_approval'.

intermediate_responses.title: Intermediate Responses
intermediate_responses.description: Progress updates during processing. Each item has: type, content, sequence, timestamp.

JobMetadata.title: JobMetadata
JobMetadata.description: Job metadata. Contents vary by job type.

job_id.title: Job Id
job_id.description: Unique job identifier. Use this to poll for status updates.

organization_id.title: Organization Id
organization_id.description: Organization that owns this job (null for user jobs).

user_id.title: User Id
user_id.description: User that owns this job (null for organization jobs).

created_at.title: Created At
created_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the job was created.

updated_at.title: Updated At
updated_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the last status change.

result.description: Job output. Present when status is 'completed'. Contains AI response and document changes for chat jobs.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error message. Present when status is 'failed'.

metadata.description: Job metadata and context. Contains pending_changes when status is 'awaiting_approval'.

JobResponse.title: JobResponse
JobResponse.description: Status and details of an async job.

JobResult.title: JobResult
JobResult.description: Job result data. Structure depends on job type.

change_id.title: Change Id
change_id.description: Unique identifier for this proposed change.

chunk_id.title: Chunk Id
chunk_id.description: Target document section ID being modified or deleted.

old_html.title: Old Html
old_html.description: Previous HTML content of the section (for updates).

new_html.title: New Html
new_html.description: Proposed new HTML content (for updates and creates).

ai_explanation.title: Ai Explanation
ai_explanation.description: AI-generated explanation of why this change was proposed.

PendingChange.title: PendingChange
PendingChange.description: A proposed document change awaiting user approval.

user_id.title: User Id
user_id.description: User who owns this session (null for organization sessions).

created_at.title: Created At
created_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the session was created.

last_activity.title: Last Activity
last_activity.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the most recent activity.

message_count.title: Message Count
message_count.description: Total number of messages in this session.

preview.title: Preview
preview.description: Preview of the first user message (up to 100 characters).

SessionInfo.title: SessionInfo
SessionInfo.description: Summary of a document editing session.

sessions.title: Sessions
sessions.description: Array of session summaries, ordered by most recent activity.

message.title: Message
message.description: Your message to the AI assistant.

session_id.title: Session Id
session_id.description: Unique session identifier. Reuse to continue a conversation.

document_html.title: Document Html
document_html.description: Current document HTML. Include data-chunk-id attributes on elements to enable targeted AI edits.

user_id.title: User Id
user_id.description: User identifier. Automatically set from authentication — typically omit this.

model_tier.title: Model Tier
model_tier.description: AI model to use: 'core' (default, fast), 'turbo' (fastest), 'pro' (advanced reasoning), 'max' (most capable).

thinking_depth.title: Thinking Depth
thinking_depth.description: Reasoning depth: 'fast', 'balanced' (default), or 'deep'. Controls how much analysis the AI performs.

approval_mode.title: Approval Mode
approval_mode.description: Change review mode: 'approve_all' (default, auto-applies changes) or 'ask_every_time' (pauses for your review).

monthly_used.title: Monthly Used
monthly_used.description: Total operations used this billing cycle.

monthly_limit.title: Monthly Limit
monthly_limit.description: Maximum operations allowed per cycle. -1 means unlimited.

monthly_remaining.title: Monthly Remaining
monthly_remaining.description: Operations remaining this cycle. -1 means unlimited.

was_billable.title: Was Billable
was_billable.description: Whether this request counted as a billable operation.

UsageInfo.title: UsageInfo
UsageInfo.description: Operation usage data for the current billing cycle.

monthly_limit.title: Monthly Limit
monthly_limit.description: Maximum operations allowed per month. -1 means unlimited.

monthly_reset_at.title: Monthly Reset At

total_sessions.title: Total Sessions

total_operations.title: Total Operations

current_month_stats.title: Current Month Stats

email_verified.title: Email Verified

monthly_operation_limit.title: Monthly Operation Limit
